title: Summary of NATO's Quantum Technologies Strategy
summary: NATO official text summarising the Alliance's first Quantum Technologies Strategy. Sets the desired end-state of transitioning NATO cryptographic systems to quantum-safe cryptography and committing Allies and NATO bodies to develop and implement post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution.

title: NATO releases first ever quantum strategy
summary: NATO news article announcing the Alliance's first quantum strategy, including the aim to upgrade and secure data communications using quantum-resistant cryptography.

title: Using quantum technologies to make communications secure
summary: NATO news article describing Alliance efforts on post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution to secure communications against the quantum threat.

title: NATO works on quantum cryptography with Malta
summary: NATO news on a Science for Peace and Security (SPS) project with Malta to establish and implement post-quantum cryptographic solutions and protocols, alongside quantum key distribution over submarine optical fibre.

title: Quantum Technologies and the Science for Peace and Security Programme
summary: NATO Science for Peace and Security Programme document on quantum technology activities, including funded post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution projects.

title: NATO Cyber Security Centre experiments with secure network capable of withstanding attack by quantum computers
issuingOrg: NCI Agency / NATO Cyber Security Centre
summary: NCI Agency / NATO Cyber Security Centre press release reporting successful testing of a hybrid post-quantum VPN combining post-quantum and traditional algorithms, with the solution proposed to the IETF for open standardisation.

title: Quantum Leap
summary: NCI Agency newsroom feature on NATO quantum-safe work, including post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution to enhance network resilience.

title: NATO Exploring Quantum Technology for Future Challenges
summary: NATO Allied Command Transformation article on quantum technology for defence, noting the need to secure digital infrastructure with quantum-resistant / post-quantum cryptography and an experiment using PQC for friend-or-foe identification of tactical drones.

title: Advanced Communication Technologies (DIANA Challenge 26-02)
summary: NATO Defence Innovation Accelerator for the North Atlantic (DIANA) challenge document featuring communication systems with integrated post-quantum cryptography.

title: Towards a Quantum-Secure Hybrid Encryptor Using Post-Quantum Cryptography and Quantum Key Distribution
summary: NATO Science and Technology Organization meeting paper proposing a hybrid encryptor combining post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution for quantum-resistant security. Authored by external researchers presented at a NATO STO symposium, so flagged for review.

title: Towards Resilient and Quantum-Safe RADAR Communication
summary: NATO Science and Technology Organization meeting paper proposing post-quantum cryptography / quantum communication for resilient military RADAR communication. Authored by external industry researchers (Terma A/S) at a NATO STO symposium, so flagged for review.

title: DISCRETION: Quantum Secure Communications for European Defence
summary: NATO Science and Technology Organization meeting paper on QKD-based quantum secure communications for European defence, framed against the post-quantum cryptography transition. Authored by external researchers at a NATO STO symposium, so flagged for review.

title: NATO Cyber Security Centre presentation on PQC migration approaches (ETSI/IQC Quantum Safe Cryptography event)
summary: Presentation by NATO Cyber Security Centre scientists (Sliwa, Wrona) on NATO post-quantum cryptography migration approaches, delivered at the ETSI/IQC Quantum Safe Cryptography event. Authored by NATO CSC but hosted on the ETSI docbox, so flagged for review.

title: On the Implementation of X509-Compliant Quantum-Safe Hybrid Certificates
summary: NATO Science and Technology Organization meeting-proceedings paper (STO-MP-IST-SET-198) presenting a proof-of-concept implementation of hybrid X.509 certificates combining a classical signature (ECDSA) with the post-quantum algorithm CRYSTALS-Dilithium via a forked OpenSSL and the Open Quantum Safe library, to build a quantum-safe hybrid PKI for military operations. Authored by external researchers presented at a NATO STO symposium, so flagged for review rather than auto-included.
